name: skill-miner
description_raw: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow. Use when David says "skill-miner", "mine my skills", "skill mining", "any skill candidates", "what should be a skill", "find skill patterns", "log this for skill-miner", "any new skills emerging", "skill miner scan". Two modes — scan (retrospective batch over recent JSONLs; default 7-day window) and log (hot-capture mid-session when David notices friction worth flagging). Patterns accumulate at ~/.claude/skill-miner/candidates.md, decay after 60+ days unseen, and promote to skill-stub drafts in promoted/ at 3+ instances within 90 days. Scoring is friction × frequency × cost, not raw repetition. Retrospective counterpart to AngelEye's live ingestion.
description_normalized: Mines David's past Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files in ~/.claude/projects/) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates that would improve his workflow.

name: ce-compound-refresh
description_raw: Refresh stale learning and pattern docs under docs/solutions/ by reviewing them against the current codebase, then updating, consolidating, or deleting drifted ones. Use when the user asks to "refresh my learnings", "audit docs/solutions/", "clean up stale learnings", or "consolidate overlapping docs", or when ce-compound flags an older doc as superseded.
description_normalized: Maintains docs/solutions/ by reviewing stale learning docs against the current codebase and updating, consolidating, or removing drifted entries.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-compound-refresh
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refresh stale learning and pattern docs under docs/solutions/

name: ce-optimize
description_raw: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops -- define a measurable goal, run parallel experiments, measure each against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, keep improvements, and converge on the best solution. Use when optimizing clustering quality, search relevance, build performance, prompt quality, or any measurable outcome that benefits from systematic experimentation.
description_normalized: Runs metric-driven iterative optimization loops with parallel experiments measured against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, converging toward the best solution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-optimize
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [path to optimization spec YAML, or describe the optimization goal]

name: kw:compound
description_raw: Extract and save learnings from a completed knowledge work session. Saves to docs/knowledge/ so future plans automatically find them.
description_normalized: Scans session for 1-3 compoundable learnings (insight/playbook/correction/pattern), requires user approval, checks for duplicates and stale entries via stale-knowledge-checker agent, then writes YAML-frontmatter markdown to docs/knowledge/.

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:compound
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extract and save learnings from a completed knowledge work session. Saves to docs/knowledge/ so future plans automatically find them.

name: stale-knowledge-checker
description_raw: After a new learning is extracted, search docs/knowledge/ for entries that the new learning contradicts or supersedes. Returns candidates for update or removal. Prevents knowledge base from accumulating contradictions.
description_normalized: Runs after kw:compound extracts a new learning; scans docs/knowledge/ for contradicted, superseded, or complementary entries and returns update/remove/keep recommendations without writing any files.

frontmatter_yaml.name: stale-knowledge-checker

name: prompt-optimizer
description_raw: Analyze raw prompts, identify intent and gaps, match ECC components (skills/commands/agents/hooks), and output a ready-to-paste optimized prompt. Advisory role only — never executes the task itself. TRIGGER when: user says "optimize prompt", "improve my prompt", "how to write a prompt for", "help me prompt", "rewrite this prompt", or explicitly asks to enhance prompt quality. Also triggers on Chinese equivalents: "优化prompt", "改进prompt", "怎么写prompt", "帮我优化这个指令". DO NOT TRIGGER when: user wants the task executed directly, or says "just do it" / "直接做". DO NOT TRIGGER when user says "优化代码", "优化性能", "optimize performance", "optimize this code" — those are refactoring/performance tasks, not prompt optimization.
description_normalized: Analyze raw prompts, identify intent and gaps, match ECC components (skills/commands/agents/hooks), and output a ready-to-paste optimized prompt.

frontmatter_yaml.name: prompt-optimizer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyze raw prompts, identify intent and gaps, match ECC components (skills/commands/agents/hooks), and output a ready-to-paste optimized prompt. Advisory role only — never executes the task itself. TRIGGER when: user says "optimize prompt", "improve my prompt", "how to write a prompt for", "help me prompt", "rewrite this prompt", or explicitly asks to enhance prompt quality. Also triggers on Chinese equivalents: "优化prompt", "改进prompt", "怎么写prompt", "帮我优化这个指令". DO NOT TRIGGER when: user wants the task executed directly, or says "just do it" / "直接做". DO NOT TRIGGER when user says "优化代码", "优化性能", "optimize performance", "optimize this code" — those are refactoring/performance tasks, not prompt optimization.

name: token-budget-advisor
description_raw: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering. Use this skill when the user explicitly wants to control response length, depth, or token budget. TRIGGER when: "token budget", "token count", "token usage", "token limit", "response length", "answer depth", "short version", "brief answer", "detailed answer", "exhaustive answer", "respuesta corta vs larga", "cuántos tokens", "ahorrar tokens", "responde al 50%", "dame la versión corta", "quiero controlar cuánto usas", or clear variants where the user is explicitly asking to control answer size or depth. DO NOT TRIGGER when: user has already specified a level in the current session (maintain it), the request is clearly a one-word answer, or "token" refers to auth/session/payment tokens rather than response size.
description_normalized: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: token-budget-advisor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Offers the user an informed choice about how much response depth to consume before answering. Use this skill when the user explicitly wants to control response length, depth, or token budget. TRIGGER when: "token budget", "token count", "token usage", "token limit", "response length", "answer depth", "short version", "brief answer", "detailed answer", "exhaustive answer", "respuesta corta vs larga", "cuántos tokens", "ahorrar tokens", "responde al 50%", "dame la versión corta", "quiero controlar cuánto usas", or clear variants where the user is explicitly asking to control answer size or depth. DO NOT TRIGGER when: user has already specified a level in the current session (maintain it), the request is clearly a one-word answer, or "token" refers to auth/session/payment tokens rather than response size.

name: writing-skills
description_raw: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment. Applies TDD to skill authoring: RED (run baseline pressure scenario, document verbatim rationalizations), GREEN (write minimal SKILL.md addressing those specific failures), REFACTOR (close loopholes, rebuild rationalization table, re-test). Iron Law: NO SKILL WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Includes Claude Search Optimization (CSO) — description = trigger only, never workflow summary.
description_normalized: Skill authoring meta-skill applying TDD Red-Green-Refactor to documentation: baseline pressure testing before writing, rationalization tables, CSO-compliant descriptions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: writing-skills
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
